design_philosophy.core_loop: Select prehistoric beast god, execute special moves via button-hold-then-joystick inputs, deplete opponent health, perform finishing move
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Combat positioning and timing combined with learning character-specific move execution patterns; managing worshipper consumption to regain health mid-match
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a prehistoric god-beast in apocalyptic arena combat; wield supernatural powers and finishing moves to dominate opponents
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Inverts standard fighting game input method (hold buttons first, then move joystick rather than motion-then-button); incorporates resource management through worshipper consumption; seven distinct character pantheon with clear moral alignment (good vs chaos/evil gods); finishing moves designed with dark humor and personality rather than pure gore

difficulty.continues_system: Single-player campaign progresses through increasing difficulty CPU opponents
difficulty.checkpointing: Campaign mode features sequential fights against CPU opponents with escalating challenge; final boss gauntlet only accessible at difficulty 10 or higher
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects difficulty level to unlock final endurance battle; minigame prior to final battle allows skill-based health replenishment through worshipper consumption

progression.upgrade_system: Pre-match minigame allows consumption of worshippers to increase health pool for endurance rounds
progression.unlock_mechanism: Difficulty level 10+ requirement unlocks final gauntlet battle against all seven fighters sequentially with powered-up health bars
replayability_hooks: Seven distinct characters with unique move sets and specialized abilities
Seven different stage environments tied to character domains
Hidden easter egg minigames (volleyball, bowling) triggerable by human-controlled player interaction
Multiple finishing moves per character with varied mechanics and humor

world_building_techniques: Post-apocalyptic setting established through stage design (meteor devastation, continental landmass reconfigured into skull shape)
Character naming and theming establishes moral pantheon (Blizzard/Armadon as peace/life gods vs Sauron/Chaos/Diablo as chaos/evil)
Human tribesmen worship creatures as gods, creating visual narrative of civilization regression and supernatural reverence
Stage-specific environments represent each character's domain (Cliff, Hollows, Inferno, etc.)
Primordial rainforest and Stone Age human tribes reinforce regressed civilization narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Final boss encounter is endurance gauntlet requiring defeat of all seven fighters sequentially with increased power; minigame prior provides skill-based resource accumulation opportunity
notable_bosses: Endurance Final Battle (difficulty 10+): Fight all seven monsters consecutively with enhanced health pools after pre-battle minigame

danger_signals: Good vs Chaos/Evil god alignment creates inherent conflict structure
Worshipper consumption mechanics establish predator-prey supernatural hierarchy
Finishing moves include transformative fatalities (e.g., Vertigo turning opponent into cow) suggesting absolute power over opponents
environmental_storytelling: Seven stage domains tied to character domains reinforce connection between god and territory
Human tribesman presence as worshippers establishes servile relationship to creatures
Post-apocalyptic Earth landscape (skull-shaped continental mass, primordial rainforest) indicates civilization collapse and natural reclamation

sources_used: wikipedia - Primal Rage game page (gameplay mechanics, character roster, plot, development, release information)
tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor Primal Rage SNES article (developer credits, technical implementation)
